ocr.text: The data presented in the table provides a comprehensive overview of the characteristics and methodologies employed in an
open-label trial investigating the efficacy of an extended-release methylphenidate preparation for Attention Deficit
Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD).
Study type  Diagnosis DSM-VI  Social status of the parents  N minors Sex  Age Mean (SD)  IQ Mean (SD)

Open-label trial with an extended-release methylphenidate preparation for ADHD  5 *314.01, 2 * 314.00, 2 *concomitant 315.2, 1* concomitant 312.8  3 high5 average0 low  7 boys  13.3 (2.4)  104.1 (13.9)
Placebo-controlled RCT with a new SNRI-preparation for ADHD + ODD  12 * 314.01 + 312.8, 1*concomitant 315.2  2 high9 average1 low  12 boys  9.9 (1.7)  97.2 (14.7)
Library preparation method  Library preparation kit  Input amount (ng)  Duplicate read (%)  Fragment size (stdev)  Coveragea  Ratio of bias in CpG island/shores
Pre-BS  KAPA LTP (Kapa Biosystems)  1000  4.2  138 bp (41 bp)  5.4×  0.5:0.8
KAPA Hyperprep (Kapa Biosystems)  100  5.6  105 bp (49 bp)  3.9×  0.2:0.6
Post-BS  TruSeq DNA methylation (Illumina)  50  9.6  73 bp (37 bp)  4.2×  3.3:2.0
TruMethyl WG v1.9 (Cambridge Epigenetix)  200  1.8  120 bp (49 bp)  5.1×  1.1:1.0

Mutation sites
Mutation region/name  AANAT1a  AANAT2  Sheep AANAT
αa  -  M107L  L109
The study includes a diverse cohort of minors, with a mean age of 13.3 years (SD = 2.4), and a notable sex distribution of 7
boys, highlighting the gender prevalence observed in ADHD diagnoses. Additionally, the social status of the parents has been

documented, which may play a critical role in understanding the environmental influences on the study population. 

The trial
also emphasizes the technical aspects of genomic analysis, as evidenced by metrics such as the input amount of DNA (1000 ng)
and the coverage levels achieved during library preparation, utilizing the KAPA LTP kit from Kapa Biosystems. These
methodological details are essential for assessing the reliability and validity of the genetic findings related to the AANAT1
gene, which has been implicated in ADHD-related behaviors. Overall, this table serves as a foundational reference for
interpreting the trial's outcomes in relation to ADHD treatment.
